title: Learned Feasibility Prediction for Robot Plans
subtitle: A feasibility predictor estimates which symbolic actions are likely to survive expensive motion checks.
summary: The TAMP project trains feasibility guidance from successful and failed traces while keeping motion checking as the verifier.

artifact.title: Guidance before verification
artifact.description: The predictor ranks or prunes plans before IK, collision, and trajectory checks spend real compute.

sections.heading: The Supervision Signal
body: Every failed IK call and collision check is useful data. A trace tells the learner what kind of partial plan was plausible symbolically but bad geometrically.

sections.heading: What It Should Not Do
body: The predictor should not declare a plan safe. It should only help decide what to try first or what to avoid trying too often.

pitfalls: Letting the predictor replace collision checking.
Optimizing median planning time while ignoring rare catastrophic failures.

quiz.question: What is the final verifier in this architecture?
options: IK, collision, trajectory, and execution checks
quiz.explanation: Learned guidance helps search, but geometric and execution checks verify feasibility.
